# ESLint & Prettier Alignment Guide

Frontloom defines quality standards that Claude follows. This guide shows how to configure ESLint and Prettier to enforce the same standards in your CI/IDE, so automated tooling and Claude stay aligned.

---

## Philosophy

Frontloom and linters are complementary:

| What | Frontloom (Claude) | ESLint / Prettier (Tooling) |
|---|---|---|
| Semantic HTML | ✓ Enforced by skill | Partial (`jsx-a11y`) |
| Accessibility | ✓ Comprehensive | Partial (`jsx-a11y`, `vuejs-accessibility`) |
| TypeScript strictness | ✓ No `any`, no unsafe casts | ✓ `@typescript-eslint` rules |
| Code formatting | Defers to project style | ✓ Prettier handles this |
| UI state completeness | ✓ Enforced by skill | ✗ Not possible to lint |
| Component API design | ✓ Enforced by skill | ✗ Not possible to lint |
| Security | ✓ Enforced by skill | Partial (`no-dangerously-set-inner-html`) |

Configure your linter to catch what machines can catch. Let Frontloom handle the judgment calls.

---

## Prettier

Frontloom doesn't enforce formatting — use whatever the project already uses. If starting fresh:

```json
// .prettierrc
{
  "semi": true,
  "singleQuote": false,
  "tabWidth": 2,
  "trailingComma": "all",
  "printWidth": 100,
  "bracketSpacing": true,
  "arrowParens": "always",
  "endOfLine": "lf"
}
```

```json
// .prettierignore
node_modules
dist
build
.next
.nuxt
.svelte-kit
.angular
coverage
```

---

## ESLint — Base Config (All Frameworks)

These rules align with Frontloom's cross-framework standards.

```js
// eslint.config.js (flat config)
import eslint from "@eslint/js";
import tseslint from "typescript-eslint";

export default tseslint.config(
  eslint.configs.recommended,
  ...tseslint.configs.strictTypeChecked,
  {
    languageOptions: {
      parserOptions: {
        projectService: true,
      },
    },
    rules: {
      // === TypeScript Strict (aligned with frontloom-core-fe) ===

      // No any
      "@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any": "error",

      // No unsafe operations
      "@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment": "error",
      "@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call": "error",
      "@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access": "error",
      "@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-return": "error",

      // No non-null assertions (prefer optional chaining)
      "@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion": "warn",

      // Prefer nullish coalescing
      "@typescript-eslint/prefer-nullish-coalescing": "error",

      // No unused variables
      "@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars": [
        "error",
        { argsIgnorePattern: "^_", varsIgnorePattern: "^_" },
      ],

      // Consistent type imports
      "@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports": [
        "error",
        { prefer: "type-imports" },
      ],

      // No enums (Frontloom prefers union types)
      "@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-types": [
        "error",
        {
          types: {
            "Function": "Use specific function types instead",
          },
        },
      ],

      // === General Quality ===

      // No console.log in production
      "no-console": ["warn", { allow: ["warn", "error"] }],

      // Prefer const
      "prefer-const": "error",

      // No var
      "no-var": "error",
    },
  },
);
```

---

## ESLint — React

```js
// eslint.config.js
import reactPlugin from "eslint-plugin-react";
import reactHooksPlugin from "eslint-plugin-react-hooks";
import jsxA11yPlugin from "eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y";

export default tseslint.config(
  // ... base config above

  // React
  reactPlugin.configs.flat.recommended,
  reactPlugin.configs.flat["jsx-runtime"],

  // React Hooks
  reactHooksPlugin.configs["recommended-latest"],

  // Accessibility (aligned with frontloom-core-fe accessibility standards)
  jsxA11yPlugin.flatConfigs.strict,

  {
    settings: {
      react: { version: "detect" },
    },
    rules: {
      // === React-specific (aligned with frontloom-react) ===

      // No array index as key
      "react/no-array-index-key": "error",

      // No dangerouslySetInnerHTML without review
      "react/no-danger": "warn",

      // Enforce self-closing tags
      "react/self-closing-comp": "error",

      // === Hooks ===

      // Exhaustive deps
      "react-hooks/exhaustive-deps": "warn",

      // === Accessibility ===

      // All images need alt
      "jsx-a11y/alt-text": "error",

      // Anchors must have content
      "jsx-a11y/anchor-has-content": "error",

      // No autofocus (can disorient screen reader users)
      "jsx-a11y/no-autofocus": "warn",

      // Click events need keyboard equivalents
      "jsx-a11y/click-events-have-key-events": "error",

      // Interactive elements need roles
      "jsx-a11y/no-static-element-interactions": "error",
    },
  },
);
```

---

## ESLint — Vue

```js
// eslint.config.js
import vuePlugin from "eslint-plugin-vue";
import vueA11yPlugin from "eslint-plugin-vuejs-accessibility";

export default tseslint.config(
  // ... base config

  ...vuePlugin.configs["flat/recommended"],
  ...vueA11yPlugin.configs["flat/recommended"],

  {
    rules: {
      // === Vue-specific (aligned with frontloom-vue) ===

      // Enforce Composition API
      "vue/component-api-style": ["error", ["script-setup"]],

      // Enforce defineModel over manual v-model
      "vue/require-macro-variable-name": "error",

      // No v-html without review
      "vue/no-v-html": "warn",

      // Enforce type-based defineProps
      "vue/define-props-declaration": ["error", "type-based"],

      // Enforce type-based defineEmits
      "vue/define-emits-declaration": ["error", "type-based"],

      // Component name casing
      "vue/component-name-in-template-casing": ["error", "PascalCase"],

      // No unused refs
      "vue/no-unused-refs": "error",

      // Enforce shorthand
      "vue/v-bind-style": ["error", "shorthand"],
      "vue/v-on-style": ["error", "shorthand"],
      "vue/v-slot-style": ["error", "shorthand"],

      // === Accessibility ===
      "vuejs-accessibility/alt-text": "error",
      "vuejs-accessibility/anchor-has-content": "error",
      "vuejs-accessibility/click-events-have-key-events": "error",
      "vuejs-accessibility/form-control-has-label": "error",
      "vuejs-accessibility/label-has-for": "error",
    },
  },
);
```

---

## ESLint — Svelte

```js
// eslint.config.js
import sveltePlugin from "eslint-plugin-svelte";
import svelteParser from "svelte-eslint-parser";

export default tseslint.config(
  // ... base config

  ...sveltePlugin.configs["flat/recommended"],

  {
    files: ["**/*.svelte"],
    languageOptions: {
      parser: svelteParser,
      parserOptions: {
        parser: tseslint.parser,
      },
    },
    rules: {
      // === Svelte-specific (aligned with frontloom-svelte) ===

      // No reactive statements ($:) in Svelte 5 — use runes
      "svelte/no-reactive-declaration": "error",

      // No stores in Svelte 5 — use runes
      "svelte/no-store": "warn",

      // Require each key
      "svelte/require-each-key": "error",

      // No target="_blank" without rel
      "svelte/no-target-blank": "error",

      // No at-html without review
      "svelte/no-at-html-tags": "warn",
    },
  },
);
```

---

## ESLint — Angular

```js
// eslint.config.js
import angularPlugin from "@angular-eslint/eslint-plugin";
import angularTemplatePlugin from "@angular-eslint/eslint-plugin-template";
import angularTemplateParser from "@angular-eslint/template-parser";

export default tseslint.config(
  // ... base config

  {
    files: ["**/*.ts"],
    plugins: { "@angular-eslint": angularPlugin },
    rules: {
      // === Angular-specific (aligned with frontloom-angular) ===

      // Enforce standalone components
      "@angular-eslint/prefer-standalone": "error",

      // Enforce OnPush change detection
      "@angular-eslint/prefer-on-push-component-change-detection": "error",

      // No empty lifecycle hooks
      "@angular-eslint/no-empty-lifecycle-method": "error",

      // Use inject() over constructor injection
      "@angular-eslint/prefer-standalone": "error",
    },
  },
  {
    files: ["**/*.html"],
    languageOptions: {
      parser: angularTemplateParser,
    },
    plugins: { "@angular-eslint/template": angularTemplatePlugin },
    rules: {
      // Accessibility in templates
      "@angular-eslint/template/alt-text": "error",
      "@angular-eslint/template/click-events-have-key-events": "error",
      "@angular-eslint/template/elements-content": "error",
      "@angular-eslint/template/label-has-associated-control": "error",
      "@angular-eslint/template/no-autofocus": "warn",
      "@angular-eslint/template/valid-aria": "error",

      // Use @for track
      "@angular-eslint/template/use-track-by-function": "error",
    },
  },
);
```

---

## ESLint — Astro

```js
// eslint.config.js
import astroPlugin from "eslint-plugin-astro";

export default tseslint.config(
  // ... base config

  ...astroPlugin.configs["flat/recommended"],
  ...astroPlugin.configs["flat/jsx-a11y-strict"],

  {
    rules: {
      // Astro-specific rules are mostly accessibility-focused
      // The astro/jsx-a11y-strict preset covers Frontloom's a11y requirements
    },
  },
);
```

---

## tsconfig.json Alignment

Frontloom requires `strict: true`. Ensure your `tsconfig.json` matches:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "strict": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "noImplicitOverride": true,
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": false,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true
  }
}
```

Key settings:
- `strict: true` — enables all strict checks (Frontloom non-negotiable #3).
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` — forces `undefined` checks on array/object access.
- `noImplicitOverride: true` — requires `override` keyword on overridden methods.

---

## Integration with CI

```yaml
# .github/workflows/lint.yml
name: Lint
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npx eslint .
      - run: npx prettier --check .
      - run: npx tsc --noEmit
```

---

## When Linter and Frontloom Disagree

If an ESLint rule conflicts with a Frontloom recommendation:

1. **Frontloom is about judgment, ESLint is about automation.** If the linter catches it, let the linter enforce it.
2. **Disable lint rules per-line only with a comment explaining why.**
3. **If you find yourself disabling the same rule repeatedly,** the rule may be wrong for your project — adjust the config, don't suppress.
4. **Frontloom covers things linters can't** (UI state completeness, component API design, architecture decisions). These are complementary, not competing.
